article_system.purpose: Rules for long-form content pages such as blog posts, legal pages, methodology pages, and informational articles.
principles: Long-form reading must optimize for comfort, clarity, and hierarchy.
Readable width, spacing, and typography are more important than decorative layout.
Article pages must feel calm, stable, and distraction-light.
The reading experience must remain excellent on both desktop and mobile.

rules: Center article content within a constrained reading column.
Do not allow article paragraphs to span excessively wide lines.
Use readable body size and generous line-height.
Paragraph line-height should typically feel more open than UI text.
Use consistent paragraph spacing.
Headings must create a clear outline.
Use generous spacing above headings, especially h2 and h3.
Headings should visually separate sections without feeling oversized.
An optional intro paragraph may use slightly larger or stronger styling.
Do not over-style intros if they reduce readability.
Keep paragraphs moderate in length.
Avoid giant text walls.
Break ideas into separate paragraphs logically.
Use lists when scanability improves.
Maintain consistent bullet spacing.
Keep list structure parallel when possible.
Visually separate quotes clearly.
Do not over-style blockquotes.
Tables inside articles must remain readable and responsive.
Use the global table system where possible.
Do not let article tables break the reading layout.
Images must scale responsively.
Use max-width 100 percent and preserve aspect ratio.
Images should not exceed the article content width.
Captions should be visually secondary and readable.
Use captions when they add meaning, not just decoration.
Use monospace styling and clear separation.
Preserve horizontal scrolling where needed instead of breaking layout.
Date, author, category, and other metadata must appear below or near the headline.
Metadata must remain visually secondary.
Metadata should be compact and scannable.
Privacy, Terms, and legal pages should use the same article container.
Use clear section breaks and headings.
Avoid inline styles.
Preserve the shared global header and footer.
Methodology content should use the same readable article structure.
Any supporting tables or lists must follow global visual standards.
Technical explanations should remain readable, not cramped.
The blog index may use a clean list or grid of article cards.
Card titles should be clear and readable.
Metadata should be secondary.
Descriptions should be short and scannable.
Optional previous/next article links should be visually secondary to the main article.
Do not clutter article pages with too many competing navigation elements.

readability_rules: Avoid overly dense side-by-side layouts inside article content.
responsive_rules: Article content width must adapt gracefully on small screens.
Images, tables, and code blocks must not break the viewport.
Metadata and header structure must remain readable on mobile.

rules: Maintain heading hierarchy correctly.
Ensure links are distinguishable.
Images must have alt text where appropriate.
Readable contrast must be preserved in article typography and metadata.

anti_patterns: Overly decorative typography in legal or informational pages
Inline styling for one-off article pages
implementation_notes_for_ai: Use a shared article-content wrapper across all long-form pages.
Preserve the same reading width and spacing rhythm throughout the site.
Prioritize reading comfort over visual novelty.
